article.body_preview:
Dhaka Chamber of Commerce and Industry (DCCI) called on the government to provide uninterrupted supply of energy to the local and export-oriented manufacturing industries and improve ease of doing business for attaining sustainable development through public-private partnership (PPP).

Mentioning the global economic downturn due to the Covid-19 pandemic and subsequent Russia-Ukraine war that have hindered Bangladesh's progressive growth, the trade body also recommended accelerating the country's

llm_connections.evidence_quote: resulting in some negative impact on the import of energy, industrial raw materials and supply chain for local export-oriented industries.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly discusses import activity at the country level, mentioning imports of energy and industrial raw materials, which matches the feature's country-wide imports scope.

verification.quote_best_match: resulting in some negative impact on the import of energy, industrial raw materials and supply chain for local export-oriented industries.
verification.judge_reasoning: The quoted text appears almost verbatim in the article: the foreign currency crisis and Taka devaluation are said to have 'resulting in some negative impact on the import of energy, industrial raw materials and supply chain for local export-oriented industries.' This explicitly states a cause-and-effect relationship at the country/economy level, matching the claimed import scope.
